meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Learn how to use SaaS pricing models to overcome the 'no project ideas' block. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for rapid growth.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for the 'perfect' idea. Instead, pick a boring B2B niche, build a simple utility tool, and implement a tiered SaaS pricing model. By focusing on monetization logic first, you force yourself to build features that solve actual business problems, effectively eliminating the 'no project ideas' paralysis forever.
content.intro:
You’re staring at a blank IDE, the cursor blinking like a heartbeat in a void. You have the technical skills to build anything, but you’re paralyzed by the 'no project ideas' syndrome. It’s the silent killer of freelance momentum, leaving you chasing tutorials instead of shipping revenue-generating products.

The secret isn't a flash of genius; it's a structural pivot. By starting with the pricing model, you shift your mindset from 'what can I build' to 'what is someone willing to pay for.' This case study shows you how to leverage AI orchestration to turn a simple pricing structure into a fully functional SaaS MVP.

steps.title: Define the Value-Based Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Before writing code, define three tiers: Free (Lead Gen), Pro (Workflow Automation), and Enterprise (Security/Customization). This dictates the feature set.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a JSON schema for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise. Include fields for feature flags, monthly costs, and annual discount percentages.
steps.claude_code_command: claude create-schema --file pricing_model.json --prompt 'Generate a SaaS pricing structure with 3 tiers'

steps.title: Architect the Subscription Logic
steps.description: Implement a robust billing interface. Use Claude Code to map your tiers to database models.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema using Prisma for a subscription-based SaaS. Include User, Subscription, and FeatureUsage models.

steps.title: Build the Stripe Integration
steps.description: Connect your pricing tiers to actual payment processing. Focus on webhooks to handle subscription lifecycle events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Stripe webhook handler in TypeScript that updates the user subscription status in the database based on invoice.payment_succeeded events.

steps.title: Develop the Pricing Dashboard
steps.description: Create a clean UI component that dynamically renders your pricing tiers based on the JSON schema created in step 1.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Build a React pricing component using Tailwind CSS that iterates through a pricing configuration object and highlights the 'Pro' tier.

steps.title: Deploy and Validate
steps.description: Use Claude Code to containerize your app and deploy it. Fast iteration is key to validating your pricing model.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js SaaS application and a deployment script for Vercel/Railway.

pro_tips: Always offer an annual plan discount of 20% to improve immediate cash flow and reduce churn.
Use feature flags to lock specific functionality for Pro users rather than just hiding the UI.
Implement a 'usage-based' cap on the free tier to nudge users toward the Pro plan.
Never hardcode prices; fetch them from your database or Stripe API to allow for A/B testing.
Automate your transactional emails for failed payments; this recovers 15-20% of lost revenue.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing is live, focus on building one single feature that justifies the 'Pro' tier price point and push it to your first 10 users.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for free and start building for value. With the right pricing architecture, your next project isn't just code—it's a business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
